accepted.reason: Pairwise distance and Gaussian affinity loops are high-signal dense numerical kernels.
Gram-Schmidt orthonormalization is a classic linear algebra workload with nested loops.
Buffon-style Monte Carlo hit counting is a compact stochastic numeric kernel.
NMS with IoU filtering is a common computer-vision optimization primitive.
Reverse-complement transformation is a widely used sequence-processing kernel.
Prefix-offset indexing and flat-index location are reusable low-level array indexing kernels.

skipped.reason: Cython language feature tests are not suitable as standalone optimization exemplars.
Advent-of-Code style script is too task-specific and limited for reusable conversion supervision.
Object-heavy ordered-structure internals are less aligned with numeric kernel optimization goals.
Small tutorial/example wrappers have limited algorithmic depth for GRPO training value.
